name: Region Analysis (admin)
description: Admin region-analysis page (/admin/region-analysis). ci-bot is a superuser, so the page renders (non-superusers are redirected to /build/workflows and the component returns null — so the h1 below ONLY exists for a confirmed superuser). The page loads annotation sets from GET /api/v1/annotations/, the list route #272 fixed (the old /annotations/sets path matched the {annotation_set_id} route with id='sets' and 500'd; see proj region-analysis live-500 fix). ci-bot's tenant has ZERO annotation sets (the API returns 200 []), so the page renders its RESOLVED empty state — NOT the loading flash ('Loading annotation sets...'), which is timing-fragile. We therefore assert the strongest stable success signal available as ci-bot: the superuser-gated h1 header AND the resolved empty-state paragraph (which renders ONLY once isLoadingSets===false, i.e. the fetch settled). A populated-data assertion (the Run Analysis / Results / History tab strip) is INFEASIBLE as ci-bot because the tenant is genuinely empty; the regression signal for the underlying API health is instead carried by the run-level same-origin HTTP-500 invariant (which would fire if /annotations/ 500'd again like the pre-#272 /annotations/sets path) plus the console-error invariant. NOTE: through the same-origin Next proxy the ci-bot annotations fetch currently resolves !response.ok and logs one console.error (the direct backend returns 200 [] — a proxy/auth quirk on this admin endpoint, flagged for follow-up); it is waived via metadata.expectedConsoleErrors so this coverage spec does not red the gate on a known, non-5xx, empty-tenant condition. Read-only; structural only.

states.name: Superuser page header
states.description: The h1 'Region Analysis' heading and the Admin breadcrumb button. The component returns null for a non-superuser (after the access-denied redirect), so the presence of this h1 confirms the page rendered for a confirmed superuser — not the redirect/blank state.

states.name: Resolved annotation-set section
states.description: The annotation-set section after the fetch settled. For ci-bot's empty tenant this is the resolved empty-state paragraph ('No annotation sets found. Run an extraction first.') plus its 'Go to Extraction' CTA. Asserting this distinguishes the RESOLVED state from the transient 'Loading annotation sets...' flash (which renders a different node with no CTA) — so a regression that leaves the page stuck loading, or that re-500s /annotations/ (caught additionally by the HTTP-500 invariant), fails this spec.

assertions.description: 'Go to Extraction' CTA — renders only in the resolved empty state, not while loading.
Resolved empty-state explanatory paragraph ('No annotation sets found…').
